headline: Long-running personal research thread on a cancer literature topic, week-by-week catch-up across devices

text: Terry — my aunt was just diagnosed with stage 2 lobular breast cancer. I want to learn what's known and what's still in trials. Start at the basics.
Save this conversation thread as 'aunt-research'. I'll come back to it across many sessions.
What's specific about lobular vs. ductal? Don't be vague.
I read that lobular is harder to detect on imaging. Why?
Save that explanation as a vault note. Audience terry only. Tag 'aunt-research'.
What are the standard-of-care treatments for stage 2 lobular? Surgery, radiation, hormone therapy, chemo — walk me through the decision tree.
When is chemo NOT recommended for stage 2? My aunt is 67.
OK Oncotype DX score — what's that and when is it run?
Save the decision-tree summary as a vault note. Same audience and tag.
Now — what's in trials right now for lobular specifically? I've heard CDK4/6 inhibitors get used in metastatic but I don't know about earlier stages.
Be honest about the line between 'in approved use' and 'in active trial'. Don't blur them.
Day 5. I switched to my desktop. Pick up the aunt-research thread.
What did we cover so far? Don't make me re-read everything — give me 5 bullets.
My aunt's oncologist suggested an aromatase inhibitor + radiation, no chemo. Score was 14. Does that match what we read?
Aromatase inhibitor side effects — what are the common ones and the rare-but-serious ones?
Save that as a 'side effects' subsection in the aunt-research notes. Don't overwrite the existing notes — append.
Bone density loss is a known issue. Is bisphosphonate co-prescription standard or controversial?
Pull the citations. I want to see actual papers, not paraphrased claims.
Save the bisphosphonate citation list as a vault note. Tag 'aunt-research' and 'citations'.
Day 10. Back on phone. Aunt-research thread.
Where did we leave off?
She's got her first AI dose tomorrow. What should she watch for in the first 2 weeks?
Add a checklist for her to a vault note titled 'aunt - first 2 weeks watch list'. Make it short and actionable.
Day 22. Desktop. Aunt-research.
She's having joint pain. Is that the AI?
What can she do about it short of stopping the medication? Don't give me 'consult your doctor' — give me what's actually known.
Save the joint-pain mitigation tactics. Tag 'aunt-research' and 'side-effects'.
What's the long-game? If AI works for her, how long does she stay on it?
Why 5 vs 10 years and what's the trade?
Save the duration discussion. Same tags.
Day 45. Phone. Quick check — what was her Oncotype score again? I forget.
Right, 14. And her current regimen?
OK. Final ask for tonight — give me the full aunt-research dossier as one document. Everything we've collected, organized.
Save it as the canonical 'aunt-research-dossier' note. Audience terry only. We're done for tonight.
